title: campanula rapunculus subvar. elatior
intro: Campanula rapunculus subvar. elatior, also known as tall bellflower, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the Campanula family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Campanula rapunculus subvar. elatior include its ability to relieve symptoms of colds, fever, and coughs, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
